notes: v1.14.41 - globe city-lights color: realism-weighted SPECTRUM mix (directive Step 3). Replaced the by-longitude landColor() gradient (which banded whole regions one hue) with a per-point random weighting: ~60% cool-white / pale-cyan (#dff6ff / #9fe6ff), ~25% violet (#9d6bff), ~15% magenta (#ff4bd8), each with small per-channel hue jitter so it reads as varied salt-and-pepper city light, not regional bands. Hub beacons untouched (separate dynamic tier - keep their magenta/violet). Builds on v1.14.40 fine point-cloud + texture crispness. JS-only (one line in the SPECTRUM seeding loop) -> CSS <style> braces unchanged 3468/3468. Gates: node --check 3/3 inline + sw.js PASS, four stamps lockstep v1.14.41, cache bumped, dct/sw CRLF + version.json LF. DEVICE-CHECK (John, iPhone, full close+reopen ONLINE to clear SW cache): city dust now reads mostly cool-white/cyan with violet + magenta accents sprinkled through (not banded by region); metros still fine + density-driven; arcs/hubs/orientation intact.
